source.name: Octave 11.1.0 simulation of Skogestad cola_lv.m + colamod.m
source.produced_by: Octave ode15s with RelTol=1e-10, AbsTol=1e-12, t in [0, 500] min, 501 uniformly spaced sample points; initial state Xinit from cola_init.mat. Reproduce by running tools/generate_skogestad_reference_trajectories.m.
citation: Skogestad, S. (1997). Dynamics and Control of Distillation Columns - A Tutorial Introduction. Trans IChemE 75(A), 539-562.
Skogestad, S. & Postlethwaite, I. (1996). Multivariable Feedback Control: Analysis and Design. Wiley.
source.role: Option-A engineering cross-check per the Phase 1 mini-gate plan. Validates that the Python port reproduces the trajectory of Skogestad's own MATLAB implementation. The port's correctness with respect to the published equations is established separately via the Skogestad-1997 Eq. (31) and Section 4.4 scalar checks (Option C) in the pytest mini-gate. Only the two product compositions y_D (X[NT-1]) and x_B (X[0]) are recorded here - they are the only quantities plotted in Skogestad's Figures 5, 6, 9 and the only ones the trajectory test compares. Tray-by-tray state validation is covered separately by the machine-precision steady-state test against cola_init.mat.

L_plus_1pct.description: +1% step in reflux LT at t=0, V/F/zF/qF held at nominal

zF_minus_10pct.description: -10% step in feed composition zF at t=0, L/V/F/qF held at nominal

F_plus_10pct.description: +10% step in feed rate F at t=0, L/V/zF/qF held at nominal
